title: Building surface atlases
text: Download annotation build two views: interactive mode, rotate surface click Use view (press U) requested view. returned camera positions can reused make later builds deterministic: interactive = FALSE camera positions supplied, ggbrat uses first n_views bundled presets.
code: aparc <- download_annotation("aparc")  atlas <- build_atlas_surf(   annot_path = aparc,   surface = "inflated",   interactive = TRUE,   n_views = 2,   view_names = c("lateral", "medial") ) atlas_again <- build_atlas_surf(   annot_path = aparc,   surface = "inflated",   camera_positions = atlas$camera_positions,   view_names = c("lateral", "medial") )

title: Building surface atlases
text: inflated surface gives clean region separation, pial surface retains anatomical structure. can blend two. See image different blend ratios.  surface_path provides control explicit FreeSurfer GIFTI files. Supply named left right paths; hemisphere can contain two paths blend.
code: atlas_blended <- build_atlas_surf(   annot_path = aparc,   surface = c("inflated", "pial"),   surf_blend_ratio = 0.7,   n_views = 2 )

what: Build a mesh from a volumetric atlas
title: Building surface atlases
text: labelled NIfTI atlas, first construct 3D mesh project . main parameters voxel_smoothing_sigma well smoothing_iterations smoothing_factor get “good” looking meshes. Generated meshes default user-specific ggbrat cache. already derived meshes many subcortical atlases, haven’t optimised parameters every one. might worth want really good results. okay half assed meshes can skip conversion build directly : add_cortex = TRUE, transparent cortical surface appears choosing camera returned cortex multipoints silhoutte can plotted “glass-brain” layer.
code: melbourne <- download_volume_atlas("Melbourne_S1")  mesh <- nifti_to_surface(   nifti_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup,   split_hemispheres = TRUE,   voxel_smoothing_sigma = 1.5,   smoothing_method = "laplacian",   smoothing_iterations = 25,   smoothing_factor = 0.2 )  subcortex <- build_atlas_surf(   mesh_path = mesh$output_file,   interactive = TRUE,   n_views = 2,   view_names = c("lateral", "medial") ) aseg_mesh <- download_surface("aseg_subcortex", type = "subcortical")  subcortex <- build_atlas_surf(   mesh_path = aseg_mesh,   add_cortex = TRUE,   interactive = TRUE,   n_views = 1 )

title: Building surface atlases
text: Set create_polygons = FALSE want projected region point clouds: keep_z_coord = TRUE, MULTIPOINT Z geometries. Z value depth chosen camera projection, original surface-space Z. polygons requested, X Y form polygons multipoint layers cortex retain depth.
code: points <- build_atlas_surf(   mesh_path = melbourne_mesh,   create_polygons = FALSE,   keep_z_coord = TRUE,   n_views = 1 )

title: Building surface atlases
text: ggbrat expects region identifier point-data array called region. imported VTP differently named array, tell builder one use: usable colour array supplied, deterministic region colours generated automatically.
code: custom <- build_atlas_surf(   mesh_path = "my_mesh.vtp",   region_array = "Label" )

title: Building surface atlases
text: See Plotting ggbrat atlases shading, joins, overlays, animation.
code: ggplot(subcortex$atlas) +   geom_sf(data = subcortex$cortex, alpha = 0.04, size = 0.05) +   geom_sf(data = subcortex$cortex_silhouette, alpha = 0.5, size = 0.5) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = color), linewidth = 0.2, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_identity() +   theme_void()

title: Building SVG atlases
text: cleanest template stores anatomical boundaries : Create one SVG group layer anatomical region. Draw region closed, filled path. Bezier freehand tools fine, provided final object ordinary closed path. fill region; stroke optional styling. Give every region stable, unique name. ggbrat checks data-name, meaningful Inkscape layer label, path <title>, finally group id. Keep paths direct children region layer. region may contain several closed paths disconnected. Repeated layers may also share region name; combine_regions = TRUE combines . convert strokes paths. produces thin compound outline rather polygon meant draw. Convert live path effects shape objects ordinary paths export. Avoid clipping masks, clones, text, embedded raster images, nested decorative groups inside atlas layers. Save plain standard SVG. Line, cubic, quadratic Bezier commands supported. Convert elliptical arcs Bezier curves.

title: Building SVG atlases
text: SVG uses downward-positive Y axis. default flip_y = TRUE converts conventional Cartesian orientation used sf ggplot.
code: atlas <- build_atlas_svg(   "my_atlas.svg",   geometry_method = "path" )  ggplot(atlas) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = region), colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +   coord_sf(datum = NA) +   theme_void()

title: Building SVG atlases
text: drawings may contain contain converted strokes sampled outlines instead closed region polygons. still possible get sf object, reconstruction, package concaveman needed. concaveman suggested rather imported reconstruction specific fallback, part normal SVG import. Smaller concavity values retain boundary detail; Inf creates convex hull.
code: install.packages("concaveman")  legacy_atlas <- build_atlas_svg(   "legacy_drawing.svg",   geometry_method = "concaveman",   concavity = 0,   length_threshold = 2.5 )

title: Building volumetric atlases
text: default, ggbrat chooses planes nearest world coordinate zero axial, sagittal, coronal axes. Choose coordinates explicitly already know slices:
code: melbourne <- download_volume_atlas("Melbourne_S1")  atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup )  ggplot(atlas) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = region), show.legend = FALSE, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.15) +   facet_wrap(~view) +   theme_void() atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup,   views = c("axial", "coronal"),   slice_coordinates = c(axial = 12, coronal = -18) )

title: Building volumetric atlases
text: finding correct coordinates hand sounds joyless, use Shiny slice selector: Switch anatomical axes, scroll volume, click Use view requested views saved. result records axis, axis-specific int_view, combined view "axial_2".  interactive selector uses suggested shiny package:
code: atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup,   interactive = TRUE,   n_views = 4 ) install.packages("shiny")

title: Building volumetric atlases
text: gray_matter_path = NULL, ggbrat resolves standard gray-matter probability map resource cache. thresholded 0.5 default. Use gray_matter_region = NA_character_ context treated atlas region. Set include_gray_matter = FALSE omit entirely.
code: atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup,   gray_matter_threshold = 0.6,   gray_matter_region = NA_character_ )

what: Smooth the two kinds of outline separately
title: Building volumetric atlases
text: Atlas regions broad gray-matter outline rarely want amount smoothing. Control independently: controls use geometric buffers simplification, round stair-step boundaries without multiplying number vertices every iteration. Fractional values supported. Start small: much smoothing can erase narrow structures.
code: atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup,   smooth_iterations = 2,   gray_matter_smooth_iterations = 0.5 )

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: load_atlas() downloads atlas necessary, stores user-specific ggbrat cache, returns R object: Premade surface atlases lists containing region polygons , available, silhouettes, shading points, cortex points, saved camera positions. get “3D look” 2D ggplot atlas, using trick. Basically, take fraction vertices original surface mesh, sampled density, plot additional layer. control strength shade adjusting size opacity multipoints. ggbrat list main atlas atlas, want add shading find multipoint geometries shade.  Note multipoints relatively computationally expensive plot, just want quickly visualize something better turn layer want render proper figure.
code: schaefer <- load_atlas("Schaefer2018_1000Parcels_7Networks_order") p1 <- ggplot(schaefer$atlas) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = color), linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_identity() +   theme_void()   p2 <- ggplot(schaefer$atlas) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = color), linewidth = 0.4, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_sf(data = schaefer$shade, size = 0.05, alpha = 0.05) +   scale_fill_identity() +   theme_void()   patchwork::wrap_plots(list(p1, p2))

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: reposition_atlas() infers plotted panels order first appear data assigns , B, C, . surface atlases, view/hemisphere combination panel. Volumetric atlases simply use distinct view values. translations applied atlas, shade, silhouette, glass-cortex layers. Use ., #, literal space empty grid cell: horizontal_gap vertical_gap arguments control space panel bounding boxes. Inspect attr(schaefer, "layout_key") see view—, applicable, hemisphere—assigned letter.
code: # Put the first four panels on one line. schaefer <- reposition_atlas(schaefer, layout = "ABCD")  # Or arrange the same panels in a two-by-two grid. schaefer <- reposition_atlas(schaefer, layout = "AB\nCD") schaefer <- reposition_atlas(schaefer, layout = "AB.\n.CD")

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: combining atlases occupy different coordinate ranges can scale one complete object get make sure comparable size. atlas_size() scales entire layout around centre, including distances views. applies transformation spatial layers atlas object, polygons, shading, silhouettes, glass-cortex points remain aligned. Values one enlarge atlas; values zero one shrink .
code:
schaefer <- load_atlas("Schaefer2018_1000Parcels_7Networks_order") yeo <- load_atlas("Yeo2011_7Networks_N1000") suit <- load_atlas("SUIT_cerebellar_lobule")  schaefer$atlas |> reposition_atlas(layout = "BACD") |>    rbind(aseg_comp$atlas |> atlas_size(2.5) |> reposition_atlas(layout = "####
CABD", horizontal_gap = 0.2))  |>    rbind(suit$atlas |> atlas_size(2.5) |> reposition_atlas(layout = "####
####
#AB") ) |>    ggplot()+   geom_sf(aes(fill = color), linewidth = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_identity() +   theme_void()

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: package includes first five cortical gradients Margulies et al. (2016), represented 1,000 parcels Schaefer atlas. use example data.
code: data("gradients")  schaefer$atlas |>   left_join(grads, by = "region") |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = gradient1), linewidth = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_sf(data = schaefer$shade, size = 0.05, alpha = 0.05) +   scale_fill_gradient2(high = "#D38A4E", low = "#3F596D") +   theme_void()

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: can even add additional layer different atlas, may useful interested brain statistics across various networks. Since polygons boundaries sit tightly shrink smooth little bit get nicer looking overlay using helper functions shrink_polygons() smooth_polygons().
code: yeo <- load_atlas("Yeo2011_7Networks_N1000") yeo$atlas <- yeo$atlas |>   shrink_polygons(dist = 0.007) |>   smooth_polygons(smoothness = 5)  schaefer$atlas |>   left_join(grads, by = "region") |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(fill = gradient1), linewidth = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_sf(data = schaefer$shade, size = 0.1, alpha = 0.075) +   geom_sf(data = yeo$atlas, aes(color = color), size = 1) +   scale_fill_gradient2(high = "#D38A4E", low = "#3F596D") +   scale_color_identity() +   theme_void()

title: Plotting ggbrat atlases
text: can also easily animate brain data using atlases, help fantastic gganimate. also showcasing another way represent cortical texture, namely multipoints instead polygons. think probably aesthetic thing likely useful scientific point view, think ’s pretty cool. makes purely pial surface actually look quite okay 2D. can generate atlases setting create_polygons FALSE.
code: schaefer1k <- download_annotation(   "annotation-schaefer2018-1000parcels-7networks-order" )  atlas_point <- build_atlas_surf(   surface = "pial",   annot_path = schaefer1k,   create_polygons = FALSE,   shade_keep_quantile = 0.4,   hemi = "right",   n_views = 1 )  library(gganimate)  animation <- atlas_point$atlas |>   left_join(     grads |> mutate(across(where(is.numeric), scale)),     by = "region"   ) |>   pivot_longer(     starts_with("grad"),     names_to = "gradient",     values_to = "values"   ) |>   ggplot() +   geom_sf(aes(color = values), size = 0.05, show.legend = FALSE) +   geom_sf(     data = atlas_point$shade,     size = 0.1,     alpha = 0.075,     color = "black"   ) +   scale_color_gradient2(high = "#D38A4E", low = "#3F596D") +   theme_void() +   transition_states(     gradient,     transition_length = 0.5,     state_length = 0.1,     wrap = TRUE   )

title: Resources and caching
text: R packages allowed large, ggbrat ships example datasets. Premade atlases, annotations, surfaces, meshes, NIfTI files downloaded demand cached locally, ready used whenever need . package includes catalog describing available resources: returned citation column gives recommended source citation every resource. Derived forms atlas—annotation, mesh, premade ggbrat RDS—share citation atlas made. [!NOTE] Provenance licensing metadata still audited resource catalog. Check original source redistributing resource. Use resource_info() want metadata particular item:
code: list_resources() list_resources(type = "atlas") resource_info("Melbourne_S1")

title: Resources and caching
text: download_atlas() returns path cached RDS file. load_atlas() reads atlas directly (downloads necessary):
code: atlas_path <- download_atlas("Yeo2011_7Networks_N1000") yeo <- load_atlas("Yeo2011_7Networks_N1000")

title: Resources and caching
text: Different builder workflows need different source files: download_annotation() cortical download_surface() calls generally return named left/right paths. volume resource contains NIfTI path paths , available, lookup table. Use "" download complete category:
code: annotation <- download_annotation("aparc") cortical_surface <- download_surface("fsaverage_inflated") subcortical_mesh <- download_surface("Melbourne_S1") volume <- download_volume_atlas("Melbourne_S1") download_annotation("all") download_atlas("all")

title: Resources and caching
text: Inspect cache location : Set different location downloading: Individual resources can removed remove_resource(). remove complete ggbrat resource cache, use clear_resource_cache(): prerelease catalog mutable. resource replaced upstream, refresh catalog force fresh download:
code: ggbrat_cache_dir() options(ggbrat.cache_dir = "/path/to/another/cache") remove_resource("aparc", type = "annotation") clear_resource_cache() atlas <- load_atlas(   "Schaefer2018_1000Parcels_7Networks_order",   refresh = TRUE,   force = TRUE )

title: Using TemplateFlow
code: templateflow_templates() templateflow_metadata("fsaverage")

title: Using TemplateFlow
text: templateflow_get() accepts TemplateFlow’s BIDS-like entities: Query left right label files separately way, adding atlas, density, desc, suffix, extension needed identify one file. broad query may validly return several paths; inspect results refine filters instead relying order.
code: left_pial <- templateflow_get(   "fsaverage",   hemi = "L",   density = "164k",   suffix = "pial",   extension = ".surf.gii" )  right_pial <- templateflow_get(   "fsaverage",   hemi = "R",   density = "164k",   suffix = "pial",   extension = ".surf.gii" )

title: Using TemplateFlow
text: blend two available geometries, query one separately pass two paths per hemisphere: TemplateFlow necessarily distribute every useful FreeSurfer surface. files available , use download_surface() local paths; build_atlas_surf() accepts interface.
code: surfaces <- c(left = left_pial, right = right_pial) labels <- c(left = left_labels, right = right_labels)  atlas <- build_atlas_surf(   surface_path = surfaces,   annot_path = labels,   interactive = TRUE,   n_views = 2,   view_names = c("lateral", "medial") ) surface_pair <- list(   left = c(left_surface_1, left_surface_2),   right = c(right_surface_1, right_surface_2) )  atlas <- build_atlas_surf(   surface_path = surface_pair,   annot_path = labels,   surf_blend_ratio = 0.7,   n_views = 2 )

title: Brain Atlases for ggplot2
text: use ggplot visualize imaging data brain atlases? Welcome ggbrat: brain atlases ggplot2! , course, already wonderful popular ggseg package particular purpose (subcortex_visualization subcortex), ggbrat offers something slightly different. ggseg subcortex_visualization provides prebuilt atlases modified geoms, focus ggbrat provide tools building 2D atlases (although also comes many prebuilt atlases). ggbrat builds two-dimensional brain atlases work directly ggplot2 sf. can turn cortical annotations, labelled (subcortical) surface meshes, volumetric NIfTI atlases, labelled SVG drawings plot-ready sf geometry, whatever view angle want. PNG snapshots outlining; automatic! origin package stemmed wanting visualize brain data using ggplot bit cortical texture (mean brain looks cool, ?). since really don’t like things manually, brain cool many different angles, developed interactive view selector can derive 3D looking 2D atlases camera angle just seconds. Example surfaces/textures (plotted using ggplot):  Example four view angles rendered 2D:  package supports many atlases file types. long surface (volume) corresponding labels (FreeSurfer, GIFTI, .vtp meshes), can kind build whatever want. example, surface rendering medial temporal lobe (shout @pyushkevich). shading region boundaries , however, better detailed mesh . Furthermore, package lets create surface meshes NIfTI atlases (’ve tested subcortical atlases yet), can build 2D atlases. example aseg atlas:  course, prefer visualize subcortex volume space, also possible. package’s slice selector lets choose whichever slices want atlas.  also presets surface-based view selector volumetric counterpart.  drawn atlas Inkscape, vector-based illustration software don’t mention name? problem! ’ve set drawing properly, can just import ready--go atlas file click button. (courtesy Anika Wuestefeld)

title: Brain Atlases for ggplot2
text: package versatility, within limits ggplot. brain imagers favoring R (perhaps ggplot), tradeoff deal . Since ggplot inherently two-dimensional plotting library, brain three-dimensional object, ggplot never able compete proper 3D rendering software terms getting sexy 3D look. However, lose sexiness gain analytical versatility. know, ggplot extremely versatile, especially numerous extensions built around , making data exploration easy compared many libraries. reason want wrap functionality around functions already handled ggplot (sf). utilities functionality support geom_sf() can used ggbrat’s atlases. get “3D look,” ggbrat can retain density-sampled fraction original mesh vertices plot additional shade layer. shade, overlays atlases, even animations still ordinary ggplot layers. See Plotting ggbrat atlases full examples. , package separates atlas creation atlas use. ggbrat atlas just Simple Features object/collection like . creating atlases (least surface-based ones) requires Python specifically vtk (trust , tried everything R, sometimes R just says “”), atlas built saved RDS file, plotting sharing ordinary R workflows require Python frankly even require package anymore. just load atlas , join data, plot (require sf though). [!NOTE] ggbrat active development. function interface downloadable resource catalog may change first stable release.

title: Brain Atlases for ggplot2
text: Install development version GitHub: Load premade atlas plot :  load_atlas() downloads atlas necessary keeps user-specific ggbrat cache next time. detail, head : Plotting ggbrat atlases Resources caching Building surface atlases Building volumetric atlases Building SVG atlases Using TemplateFlow Function reference Plotting premade atlases, downloading resources, using volumetric SVG functionality require R . Surface projection NIfTI--mesh conversion additionally require Python 3.9 newer. reticulate >= 1.41, ggbrat declares required Python packages functions first used.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jorittmo/ggbrat") library(ggbrat) library(ggplot2)  yeo <- load_atlas("Yeo2011_7Networks_N1000")  ggplot(yeo$atlas) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = color), linewidth = 0.15) +   geom_sf(data = yeo$shade, size = 0.1, alpha = 0.1) +   scale_fill_identity() +   theme_void()

what: Build 2D atlas views from cortical or labelled surface meshes — brain_views
title: Build 2D atlas views from cortical or labelled surface meshes — brain_views
text: brain_views() package-facing orchestration layer 2D atlas workflow. default expects pre-recorded camera presets atlas builds deterministic. Set interactive = TRUE need record new camera positions custom view. positioning mesh, click green Use view control press U save camera close viewer.

title: Build 2D atlas views from cortical or labelled surface meshes — brain_views
code: brain_views(   annot_path = NULL,   hemi = c("both", "left", "right"),   n_views = NULL,   view_names = NULL,   camera_positions = NULL,   interactive = FALSE,   surf_dir = NULL,   surface = "pial",   surface_path = NULL,   surf_blend_ratio = NULL,   window_size = c(800L, 600L),   include_silhouette = FALSE,   sil_decimate = 0.1,   silhouette_min_length = "auto",   silhouette_tolerance = "auto",   mesh_path = NULL,   region_array = "region",   color_array = "color",   mesh_hemisphere = "subcortical",   add_cortex = FALSE,   cortex_surf_dir = surf_dir,   cortex_surface = "pial",   cortex_surface_path = NULL,   cortex_point_method = c("density", "sample", "all"),   cortex_point_fraction = 0.05,   cortex_density_k = 15L,   cortex_density_keep_quantile = 0.15,   cortex_max_points = 10000L,   include_cortex_silhouette = TRUE,   cortex_preview_opacity = 0.1,   keep_z_coord = FALSE,   label_array = NULL )

title: Build 2D atlas views from cortical or labelled surface meshes — brain_views
text: annot_path cortical surfaces, path single .annot .label.gii file, named vector/list left right. May NULL mesh_path supplied. hemi Hemisphere process cortical surfaces. n_views Number views build. camera_positions supplied, defaults length(camera_positions). view_names Optional names views. camera_positions Optional list saved PyVista camera positions. NULL non-interactive mode, bundled presets used first n_views presets selected. interactive Whether capture camera positions interactively. surf_dir Directory containing FreeSurfer surface files. NULL, requested fsaverage surfaces downloaded resolved user-specific ggbrat cache. surface single surface name pair surfaces blend. surface_path Optional named left/right paths FreeSurfer .surf.gii files. Supply named list hemisphere contains two paths blend explicit files. Overrides surf_dir surface. surf_blend_ratio Weight assigned first surface surface surface_path hemisphere contains two surfaces. second surface receives weight 1 - surf_blend_ratio. window_size PyVista window size. include_silhouette Whether compute return silhouettes. sil_decimate Fraction silhouette polyline points remove. silhouette_min_length Minimum retained silhouette-path length, "auto" approximately one output pixel. silhouette_tolerance Line-simplification tolerance, "auto" approximately half output pixel. mesh_path Optional path labelled VTK/VTP mesh, named pair elements left right. pair follows mirrored-camera workflow cortical hemispheres; single mesh rendered per view. region_array Name mesh point-data array containing region identifiers. Set another one-component point array, "Label", importing mesh contain "region". color_array Optional mesh point-data array containing region colors. absent, deterministic colors generated automatically. mesh_hemisphere Value stored output hemisphere column generic mesh. add_cortex Whether add separately projected fsaverage surfaces glass-brain context layer. currently requires paired meshes. cortex_surf_dir Directory containing left right FreeSurfer cortical surfaces. default uses surf_dir. cortex_surface FreeSurfer surface name used glass layer. cortex_surface_path Optional named left/right paths FreeSurfer GIFTI surfaces glass layer. hemisphere may alternatively contain two paths blend. Overrides cortex_surf_dir cortex_surface. cortex_point_method visible cortical points retained: "density" keeps projected structural concentrations, "sample" takes reproducible random sample, "" retains every visible vertex. cortex_point_fraction Fraction visible cortical vertices retained cortex_point_method = "sample". cortex_density_k Number neighbors used projected cortical density estimation. cortex_density_keep_quantile Fraction densest projected cortical vertices retained. cortex_max_points Maximum density-filtered points retained per hemisphere view. Use NULL cap. include_cortex_silhouette Whether return cortical outline layer. cortex_preview_opacity Opacity cortical surfaces shown interactively choosing camera. preview participate target-mesh visibility calculations. keep_z_coord Whether visible vertices retain projected depth third coordinate. returned geometry MULTIPOINT Z; Z display depth selected camera projection, original surface-space Z. label_array Deprecated alias region_array.

title: Build 2D atlas views from cortical or labelled surface meshes — brain_views
text: list atlas, optional silhouette, camera_positions, , requested, cortex cortex_silhouette glass-brain layers.

what: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
title: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
text: helper wraps full atlas-building pipeline: visible vertex extraction, view shifting, region polygon creation, shade geometry creation.

title: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
code: build_atlas_surf(   annot_path = NULL,   hemi = c("both", "left", "right"),   n_views = NULL,   view_names = NULL,   camera_positions = NULL,   interactive = FALSE,   surf_dir = NULL,   surface = "pial",   surface_path = NULL,   surf_blend_ratio = NULL,   window_size = c(800L, 600L),   include_silhouette = FALSE,   sil_decimate = 0.1,   silhouette_min_length = "auto",   silhouette_tolerance = "auto",   smoothing_factor = 5,   create_polygons = TRUE,   keep_z_coord = FALSE,   shade_k = 15,   shade_keep_quantile = 0.2,   cell_dx = 1.5,   cell_dy = -1,   n_cols = 2,   mesh_path = NULL,   region_array = "region",   color_array = "color",   mesh_hemisphere = "subcortical",   add_cortex = FALSE,   cortex_surf_dir = surf_dir,   cortex_surface = "pial",   cortex_surface_path = NULL,   cortex_point_method = c("density", "sample", "all"),   cortex_point_fraction = 0.05,   cortex_density_k = 15L,   cortex_density_keep_quantile = 0.15,   cortex_max_points = 50000L,   include_cortex_silhouette = TRUE,   cortex_preview_opacity = 0.1,   label_array = NULL )

title: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
text: annot_path cortical surfaces, path single .annot .label.gii file, named vector/list left right. May NULL mesh_path supplied. hemi Hemisphere process cortical surfaces. n_views Number views build. camera_positions supplied, defaults length(camera_positions). view_names Optional names views. camera_positions Optional list saved PyVista camera positions. NULL non-interactive mode, bundled presets used first n_views presets selected. interactive Whether capture camera positions interactively. surf_dir Directory containing FreeSurfer surface files. NULL, requested fsaverage surfaces downloaded resolved user-specific ggbrat cache. surface single surface name pair surfaces blend. surface_path Optional named left/right paths FreeSurfer .surf.gii files. Supply named list hemisphere contains two paths blend explicit files. Overrides surf_dir surface. surf_blend_ratio Weight assigned first surface surface surface_path hemisphere contains two surfaces. second surface receives weight 1 - surf_blend_ratio. window_size PyVista window size. include_silhouette Whether compute return silhouettes. sil_decimate Fraction silhouette polyline points remove. silhouette_min_length Minimum retained silhouette-path length, "auto" approximately one output pixel. silhouette_tolerance Line-simplification tolerance, "auto" approximately half output pixel. smoothing_factor Scaling factor used ashape_polygon_sf(). create_polygons Whether convert region's projected MULTIPOINT geometry polygon. FALSE, region multipoints returned unchanged. keep_z_coord Whether retain projected display depth third coordinate returned multipoints. create_polygons = TRUE, region polygons constructed X Y , multipoint layers cortex retain Z. shade_k Number neighbours used knn_density_filter() shade creation. shade_keep_quantile Quantile dense points kept shade creation. cell_dx Horizontal spacing shifted view cells. cell_dy Vertical spacing shifted view cells. n_cols Number columns shifted view grid. mesh_path Optional path labelled VTK/VTP mesh, named pair elements left right. pair follows mirrored-camera workflow cortical hemispheres; single mesh rendered per view. region_array Name mesh point-data array containing region identifiers. Set another one-component point array, "Label", importing mesh contain "region". color_array Optional mesh point-data array containing region colors. absent, deterministic colors generated automatically. mesh_hemisphere Value stored output hemisphere column generic mesh. add_cortex Whether add separately projected fsaverage surfaces glass-brain context layer. currently requires paired meshes. cortex_surf_dir Directory containing left right FreeSurfer cortical surfaces. default uses surf_dir. cortex_surface FreeSurfer surface name used glass layer. cortex_surface_path Optional named left/right paths FreeSurfer GIFTI surfaces glass layer. hemisphere may alternatively contain two paths blend. Overrides cortex_surf_dir cortex_surface. cortex_point_method visible cortical points retained: "density" keeps projected structural concentrations, "sample" takes reproducible random sample, "" retains every visible vertex. cortex_point_fraction Fraction visible cortical vertices retained cortex_point_method = "sample". cortex_density_k Number neighbors used projected cortical density estimation. cortex_density_keep_quantile Fraction densest projected cortical vertices retained. cortex_max_points Maximum density-filtered points retained per hemisphere view. Use NULL cap. include_cortex_silhouette Whether return cortical outline layer. cortex_preview_opacity Opacity cortical surfaces shown interactively choosing camera. preview participate target-mesh visibility calculations. label_array Deprecated alias region_array.

title: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
text: list containing shifted atlas output atlas, optional silhouette, camera_positions, shade, optional cortex cortex_silhouette glass-brain layers. atlas contains polygons create_polygons = TRUE region multipoints otherwise.

title: Build a shifted polygon atlas with shading support — build_atlas_surf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ melbourne_mesh <- download_surface("Melbourne_S1", type = "subcortical") subcortical_atlas <- build_atlas_surf(   mesh_path = melbourne_mesh,   add_cortex = TRUE,   n_cols = 2 ) } # }

what: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
text: build_atlas_svg() reads SVG groups containing paths, samples line Bezier geometry, creates one polygon per labelled region. Exact closed paths preserved; hull reconstruction remains available legacy SVGs whose paths represent converted strokes rather regions.

title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
code: build_atlas_svg(   svg_path,   curve_steps = 20L,   geometry_method = c("auto", "path", "concaveman"),   concavity = 0,   length_threshold = 2.5,   flip_y = TRUE,   combine_regions = TRUE )

title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
text: svg_path Path SVG file. curve_steps Number straight segments used approximate Bezier curve. geometry_method Geometry construction method. "path" preserves authored boundary, "concaveman" reconstructs boundary sampled points, "auto" uses exact paths labelled region drawings concaveman legacy ID-drawings. concavity Concaveman concavity parameter. Smaller values produce detailed boundary; Inf produces convex hull. length_threshold Concaveman segment-length threshold. Segments shorter value refined . flip_y Whether convert SVG's downward-positive Y coordinates conventional Cartesian coordinates. combine_regions Whether groups region name combined single (possibly multipart) feature.

title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
text: sf object region geometry columns.

title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
text: New templates encode anatomical boundaries directly instead relying hull reconstruction: Create one SVG group/layer anatomical region. Draw region closed, filled path. Either Bezier tool freehand tool suitable, provided final object ordinary closed path. fill represents region; stroke optional styling around boundary. Give region stable, unique name. Names resolved data-name, meaningful Inkscape layer label, path <title>, finally group id. Generic labels Layer 2 ignored path title available. Keep path direct child region layer. layer may contain multiple closed paths disconnected region. Alternatively, repeated layers can use region name; combine_regions = TRUE combines one multipart feature. convert strokes paths. creates thin compound outline rather anatomical polygon requires reconstruction. Convert live path effects shape objects ordinary paths final export. Avoid clipping masks, clones, text, embedded raster images, nested decorative groups atlas layers. Save plain standard SVG. Line, cubic quadratic Bezier path commands supported. Convert elliptical arcs Bezier curves. importer supports translate, scale, rotate, matrix transforms. Import new templates geometry_method = "path". "auto" method treats path <title> exact-region marker; ID-legacy drawings reconstructed concaveman. Since concaveman optional suggested dependency, install legacy reconstruction needed install.packages("concaveman"). SVG coordinates downward-positive Y axis. flip_y = TRUE converts conventional Cartesian orientation used sf ggplot2.

title: Build an sf atlas from labelled SVG layers — build_atlas_svg
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Select an SVG whose labelled groups contain the atlas regions. atlas <- build_atlas_svg(   svg_path = file.choose(),   geometry_method = "path" )  # Only for legacy drawings whose paths are converted stroke outlines: legacy_atlas <- build_atlas_svg(   svg_path = file.choose(),   geometry_method = "concaveman",   concavity = 0,   length_threshold = 2.5 ) } # }

what: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
text: build_atlas_vol() combines discrete-label subcortical atlas thresholded gray-matter anatomical context image converts orthogonal slices sf polygons. Region boundaries polygonized directly raster cells; hull reconstruction performed.

title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
code: build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path,   lookup_path = NULL,   gray_matter_path = NULL,   gray_matter_threshold = 0.5,   views = c("axial", "sagittal", "coronal"),   slice_coordinates = c(axial = 0, sagittal = 0, coronal = 0),   labels = NULL,   include_gray_matter = TRUE,   gray_matter_region = "gray_matter",   exclude_atlas_from_gray_matter = TRUE,   smooth_iterations = 0,   gray_matter_smooth_iterations = smooth_iterations,   interactive = FALSE,   n_views = NULL )

title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
text: atlas_path Path discrete-label NIfTI atlas. lookup_path Optional CSV whose first two columns contain numeric labels region names. Headerless files supported. gray_matter_path Path gray-matter probability anatomical context NIfTI. NULL, MNI152NLin2009cAsym gray-matter probability map downloaded resolved ggbrat cache. gray_matter_threshold Values greater equal threshold included context mask. views ordered subset "axial", "sagittal", "coronal". slice_coordinates Named world coordinates requested views. Defaults coordinate zero every view. labels Optional numeric atlas labels include. default includes every nonzero label intersecting selected slice. include_gray_matter Whether include thresholded context region. gray_matter_region Region name assigned context polygons. May NA_character_ context treated atlas region. exclude_atlas_from_gray_matter Whether atlas-labelled cells removed context mask returned polygons form nonoverlapping categorical atlas. smooth_iterations Strength geometric outline smoothing, expressed voxel-width steps. Zero (default) preserves exact voxel boundaries; values 1 3 progressively round atlas-region stair-step edges. Fractional values supported. Smoothing uses morphological buffers followed simplification repeatedly multiply number boundary vertices. gray_matter_smooth_iterations Smoothing strength gray-matter context outline. default inherits smooth_iterations; set separately cortex needs less smoothing atlas regions. interactive TRUE, open interactive slice selector. user can switch anatomical axes, scroll slices, save n_views selections polygon construction begins. Requires suggested package shiny. n_views Number slices save interactive mode.

title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
text: sf object region polygons columns region, label, tissue, axis, int_view, view, selection_order, slice_index, slice_coordinate, requested_coordinate. int_view numbers slices within anatomical axis view combines axis number, example "axial_2". Use ggplot2::facet_wrap(~view) display views separately.

title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
text: default, nearest voxel plane world coordinate zero selected axial, sagittal, coronal views. input images must currently share dimensions voxel--world affine transformation.

title: Build orthogonal 2D atlases from volumetric NIfTI images — build_atlas_vol
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ melbourne <- download_volume_atlas("Melbourne_S1") atlas <- build_atlas_vol(   atlas_path = melbourne$nifti,   lookup_path = melbourne$lookup )  ggplot2::ggplot(atlas) +   ggplot2::geom_sf(ggplot2::aes(fill = region), linewidth = 0.2) +   ggplot2::facet_wrap(~view) +   ggplot2::theme_void() } # }

NULL, requested fsaverage surfaces downloaded resolved user-specific ggbrat cache. surface single surface name pair surfaces blend. surface_path Optional named left/right paths FreeSurfer .surf.gii files. Overrides surf_dir surface. surf_blend_ratio Weight assigned first surface surface surface_path hemisphere contains two surfaces. second surface receives weight 1 - surf_blend_ratio. window_size PyVista window size. sil_decimate Fraction silhouette polyline points remove. mesh_path Optional path labelled VTK/VTP mesh, named pair elements left right. pair follows mirrored-camera workflow cortical hemispheres; single mesh rendered per view. region_array Name mesh point-data array containing region identifiers. what: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
title: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
text: Parcel-level values first five cortical connectivity gradients Margulies et al. (2016), represented 1,000 parcels Schaefer 2018 atlas using seven-network region names. row corresponds one Schaefer parcel.

title: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
code: grads

title: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
text: tibble 1,000 rows 6 columns: gradient1 Numeric score first connectivity gradient. gradient2 Numeric score second connectivity gradient. gradient3 Numeric score third connectivity gradient. gradient4 Numeric score fourth connectivity gradient. gradient5 Numeric score fifth connectivity gradient. region Character Schaefer-1000 parcel name using seven-network naming scheme.

title: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
text: Margulies, D. S., Ghosh, S. S., Goulas, ., et al. (2016). Situating default-mode network along principal gradient macroscale cortical organization. Proceedings National Academy Sciences, 113(44), 12574-12579. doi:10.1073/pnas.1608282113

title: Five cortical connectivity gradients for the Schaefer 1000 atlas — grads
text: data stored data/gradients.rda; R object loaded data() named grads. Gradient direction sign-indeterminate underlying decomposition, interpretation focus relative positions along gradient unless orientation explicitly checked source representation.

what: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
title: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
text: Creates nonzero atlas region independently using marching cubes, combines disconnected components one VTP file. numeric label character region arrays stored point cell data.

title: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
code: nifti_to_surface(   nifti_path,   lookup_path = NULL,   output_file = NULL,   labels = NULL,   split_hemispheres = FALSE,   mask_threshold = 0.5,   surface_style = c("faithful", "display"),   minimum_component_voxels = 2L,   closing_iterations = 1L,   fill_voxel_holes = TRUE,   distance_upsampling = 2L,   preserve_volume = TRUE,   small_region_method = c("ellipsoid", "mesh"),   small_region_threshold = 20L,   topology_correction = c("none", "genus0"),   closing_radius = 1,   max_closing_radius = 2,   reduction = 0,   subdivision = 0L,   voxel_smoothing_sigma = 0,   smoothing_method = c("windowed_sinc", "laplacian"),   smoothing_iterations = 0L,   smoothing_factor = 0.1,   minimum_vertices = 4L,   minimum_volume = 0.01,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
text: nifti_path Path discrete-label .nii .nii.gz image, directory standalone region images, character vector standalone region images. lookup_path Optional CSV whose first two columns contain numeric labels region names. Headerless files supported. output_file Destination .vtp file. default named NIfTI image ggbrat user cache's generated/surfaces directory. labels Optional numeric labels include. default uses every nonzero label image. split_hemispheres Whether create separate _left.vtp _right.vtp meshes. Hemispheres inferred region-name components _L, _R, left, right, lh, rh lookup table. mask_threshold standalone images, voxel values greater equal threshold included region mask. surface_style Standalone-mask surface style. "faithful" preserves input mask; "display" enables cleanup volume-preserving display geometry. minimum_component_voxels Display-mode voxel islands smaller removed. closing_iterations Display-mode binary-closing iterations. fill_voxel_holes Whether display mode fills enclosed voxel holes. distance_upsampling Display-mode signed-distance upsampling factor. preserve_volume Whether display-mode meshes rescaled cleaned mask volume smoothing. small_region_method Either "ellipsoid" "mesh" small standalone masks. small_region_threshold Masks fewer voxels use selected small region method. topology_correction Standalone display-mode topology correction. "genus0" adaptively closes tunnels; "none" preserves topology. closing_radius Initial spherical closing radius world-coordinate units topology correction. max_closing_radius Maximum adaptive spherical closing radius. reduction Proportion triangles remove region, [0, 1). combined subdivision. subdivision Number linear subdivision levels 0 3. level creates approximately four times many triangles. adds sampling vertices add anatomical detail beyond NIfTI. voxel_smoothing_sigma Standard deviation, voxels, Gaussian smoothing label's signed-distance field marching cubes. Zero preserves exact voxel-derived boundary; values around 0.5 1.5 progressively reduce staircase appearance. smoothing_method Either "windowed_sinc", better preserves volume, "laplacian", strongly rounds voxel edges. smoothing_iterations Number windowed-sinc smoothing iterations. smoothing_factor Smoothing strength (0, 1]: relaxation factor Laplacian smoothing passband windowed-sinc smoothing. minimum_vertices Connected components fewer vertices removed. minimum_volume Connected components smaller volume cubic world-coordinate units removed. overwrite Whether existing output file may replaced.

title: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
text: list containing output_file, per-region mesh summaries, total vertex face counts.

title: Create a labelled surface mesh from a NIfTI atlas — nifti_to_surface
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ brainstem_files <- download_volume_atlas("Brainstem_Navigator") brainstem <- nifti_to_surface(   nifti_path = brainstem_files$nifti,   lookup_path = brainstem_files$lookup ) } # }

what: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
text: Arranges atlas panels using compact design string. Panels inferred atlas order first appear assigned letters , B, C, . surface atlas, panel unique view hemisphere combination. atlas without multiple hemispheres, including volumetric atlas, panel unique view.

title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
code: reposition_atlas(x, layout, horizontal_gap = 0.05, vertical_gap = 0.05)

title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
text: x sf atlas, list returned build_atlas_surf(). layout single character string describing grid. non-empty cell must letter assigned one inferred panel. Newlines separate rows; ., #, spaces denote empty cells. horizontal_gap Horizontal distance adjacent view bounding boxes, coordinate units atlas. vertical_gap Vertical distance adjacent view bounding boxes, coordinate units atlas.

title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
text: object form x, repositioned geometries. data frame mapping layout letters panel metadata stored layout_key attribute.

title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
text: example, "ABCD" places four panels one row, "AB\nCD" creates two--two grid. Use ., #, space empty cell. Letter matching case-insensitive. x list returned build_atlas_surf(), translations applied atlas, shade, silhouette, cortex, cortex_silhouette layers. Camera positions non-spatial components left unchanged.

title: Reposition atlas panels with a text-based layout — reposition_atlas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ atlas <- load_atlas("Schaefer2018_400Parcels_7Networks_order")  # Put four panels in one row. atlas <- reposition_atlas(atlas, layout = "ABCD")  # Put the same panels in a two-by-two grid. atlas <- reposition_atlas(atlas, layout = "AB\nCD")  # Inspect which panel was assigned to each letter. attr(atlas, "layout_key") } # }

what: Inspect the ggbrat resource catalog — resource_catalog
title: Inspect the ggbrat resource catalog — resource_catalog
text: package ships catalog snapshot. Set refresh = TRUE read current catalog mutable resources prerelease.

title: Inspect the ggbrat resource catalog — resource_catalog
code: resource_catalog(refresh = FALSE, quiet = FALSE)

title: Inspect the ggbrat resource catalog — resource_catalog
text: refresh Whether download current remote catalog. quiet Whether suppress download progress.

title: Inspect the ggbrat resource catalog — resource_catalog
text: data frame containing one row per resource, including recommended source citation. associated file table stored files attribute.

what: Show metadata for a ggbrat resource — resource_info

text: Show metadata ggbrat resource

title: Show metadata for a ggbrat resource — resource_info
code: resource_info(name, type = NULL, refresh = FALSE)

title: Show metadata for a ggbrat resource — resource_info
text: name Resource name, id, vector names, "". type Optional resource category. refresh Whether refresh mutable remote catalog.

title: Show metadata for a ggbrat resource — resource_info
text: Matching catalog rows.

what: Shift atlas views into a plotting grid — shift_brain_views
title: Shift atlas views into a plotting grid — shift_brain_views
text: Shift atlas views plotting grid

title: Shift atlas views into a plotting grid — shift_brain_views
code: shift_brain_views(sf_obj, cell_dx = 1.5, cell_dy = -1, n_cols = 2)

title: Shift atlas views into a plotting grid — shift_brain_views
text: sf_obj sf object hemisphere view columns, raw list returned brain_views(). cell_dx Horizontal spacing cells. cell_dy Vertical spacing cells. n_cols Number columns output grid.

title: Shift atlas views into a plotting grid — shift_brain_views
text: sf object shifted geometry, raw atlas list shifted atlas optional shifted silhouette.

title: Shrink polygon geometries — shrink_polygons
text: Applies negative buffer sf object repairing invalid geometries. useful creating small visual gap adjacent atlas regions. Features may become empty dist large relative polygon.

title: Shrink polygon geometries — shrink_polygons
code: shrink_polygons(x, dist = 0.005)

title: Shrink polygon geometries — shrink_polygons
text: x sf object containing polygon multipolygon geometries. dist Non-negative buffer distance used shrink polygon. value interpreted coordinate units x; absolute value used.

title: Shrink polygon geometries — shrink_polygons
text: sf object inward-buffered geometries attribute columns x.

title: Shrink polygon geometries — shrink_polygons
code: square <- sf::st_sf(   region = "example",   geometry = sf::st_sfc(sf::st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE   )))) ) smaller <- shrink_polygons(square, dist = 0.05)

what: Convert silhouette segments into merged sf lines — silhouette_sf
title: Convert silhouette segments into merged sf lines — silhouette_sf
text: Convert silhouette segments merged sf lines Backward-compatible alias earlier misspelled helper

title: Convert silhouette segments into merged sf lines — silhouette_sf
code: silhouette_sf(   sil,   min_length = "auto",   simplify_tolerance = "auto",   window_size = c(800L, 600L) )  silhoutte_sf(   sil,   min_length = "auto",   simplify_tolerance = "auto",   window_size = c(800L, 600L) )

title: Convert silhouette segments into merged sf lines — silhouette_sf
text: sil Data frame columns x0, y0, x1, y1. min_length Minimum retained path length, "auto" one pixel. simplify_tolerance Simplification tolerance, "auto" half pixel. window_size Render window dimensions used automatic thresholds.

title: Convert silhouette segments into merged sf lines — silhouette_sf
text: sf object.

title: Smooth polygon boundaries — smooth_polygons
text: Smooths geometries sf object using smoothr::smooth(). Attribute columns retained. available methods interpretation smoothness defined smoothr.

title: Smooth polygon boundaries — smooth_polygons
code: smooth_polygons(x, method = "ksmooth", smoothness = 3)

title: Smooth polygon boundaries — smooth_polygons
text: x sf object containing polygon multipolygon geometries. method Smoothing method passed smoothr::smooth(). Common choices include "ksmooth", "chaikin", "spline", "densify". smoothness Smoothing parameter passed selected method smoothr::smooth().

title: Smooth polygon boundaries — smooth_polygons
text: sf object smoothed geometries attribute columns x.

title: Smooth polygon boundaries — smooth_polygons
code: square <- sf::st_sf(   region = "example",   geometry = sf::st_sfc(sf::st_polygon(list(matrix(     c(0, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1, 0, 1, 0, 0), ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE   )))) ) smoothed <- smooth_polygons(square, method = "ksmooth", smoothness = 3)

title: Query resources from TemplateFlow — templateflow
text: Thin R wrappers around official TemplateFlow Python client. Resources downloaded lazily TemplateFlow's cache returned local paths. functions require Python called.

title: Query resources from TemplateFlow — templateflow
code: templateflow_templates(...)  templateflow_get(template, ...)  templateflow_metadata(template)  templateflow_citations(template, bibtex = FALSE)

title: Query resources from TemplateFlow — templateflow
text: ... BIDS-like TemplateFlow query entities atlas, hemi, density, resolution, desc, suffix, extension. template TemplateFlow template identifier, without tpl- prefix. bibtex Whether citations returned BibTeX form.

title: Query resources from TemplateFlow — templateflow
text: templateflow_templates() returns template identifiers; templateflow_get() returns local file paths; metadata citation functions return values supplied TemplateFlow.
